task_completion.description: Open the docker-compose.yml file in a text editor
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor (like nano, vim, or VS Code) is open showing the contents of /home/ga/legacy-app/docker-compose.yml.

task_completion.description: Add the WAF service using the specified image
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file contains a new service (e.g., 'waf') with the image specified as 'owasp/modsecurity-crs:3-nginx'.

task_completion.description: Configure the BACKEND environment variable for the WAF
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WAF service in docker-compose.yml includes an environment variable 'BACKEND' pointing to the app service (e.g., 'http://app:5000').

task_completion.description: Update port mappings to make the WAF the exclusive entry point
task_completion.visual_evidence: The docker-compose.yml file shows host port '8080' mapped to the WAF service (e.g., '8080:80' or '8080:8080'), and the 'app' service no longer has port 8080 mapped to the host.

task_completion.description: Restart the docker-compose services to apply changes
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal shows a command like 'docker-compose up -d' or 'docker compose up -d' being executed in the /home/ga/legacy-app directory.

task_completion.description: Verify that the WAF blocks SQL injection and allows legitimate traffic
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal or browser shows a test against http://localhost:8080. A legitimate query (e.g., '?q=Alice') should succeed, and a SQL injection attempt (e.g., '?q=' OR 1=1') should return a 403 Forbidden error.

integrity.description: The agent actually modifies the docker-compose.yml file
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses a real text editor to modify the file, rather than just echoing text to the console or creating a fake file.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely tests the application endpoints
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent uses curl commands or a web browser to send requests to http://localhost:8080, rather than just printing the expected 403 Forbidden output.

integrity.description: The agent does not modify the underlying application code to fix the vulnerability
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent only modifies the docker-compose.yml file and does not edit the Python/Flask application files to patch the SQL injection directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires modifying /home/ga/legacy-app/docker-compose.yml to add a WAF sidecar. The WAF service must use the 'owasp/modsecurity-crs:3-nginx' image. The WAF must proxy traffic to the internal app service running on port 5000, so the BACKEND environment variable should be set to 'http://app:5000'. The WAF must be the exclusive entry point on host port 8080, meaning the port mapping '8080:80' (or similar) should be on the WAF service, and the app service should not expose port 8080 directly. Legitimate queries to http://localhost:8080 should return 200 OK, while SQL injection attempts (e.g., '?q=' OR 1=1') should be blocked by the WAF with a 403 Forbidden response.
